findings.message: The plan intentionally adds more manual data to site-content.generated.json while leaving the generator untouched. That file already carries a _manualExtension warning, and the materializer rewrites the payload wholesale. As written, the new chrome block will be lost on the next regeneration, so this is not a durable source-of-truth design.

TASK-02 says layout will pass strings to NotifyMeForm, but NotifyMeForm is actually rendered from product/[slug]/page.tsx, not from layout.tsx. The task's deliverable/affects lists omit the product page entirely.
The proposed chrome schema misses existing hardcoded accessibility strings in scope, including aria-label='Primary' in Header.tsx and aria-label='Footer' in SiteFooter.tsx.

NotifyMeForm will render localized error messages, but /api/notify-me returns English strings like 'Consent required' and 'Invalid email' from route.ts. Without localizing API responses or mapping server error codes to locale-specific client copy, non-EN users will still see English validation errors.
The validation command is written as pnpm typecheck --filter caryina, but the repo runbook requires pnpm --filter <pkg> typecheck.
